compositional_truth_check.findings: P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) and P1 (String of Nickels) are present. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are present but show signs of severe decline or dormancy.

inventory_reconciliation.p2_p4: Present (Black pot, shared, showing extreme senescence)
inventory_reconciliation.p3: Present (Black pot, Pothos, White Rabbit anchor)
inventory_reconciliation.systemic_losses: None confirmed, though P2/P4 are in critical condition.
inventory_reconciliation.new_introductions: Eggshell fragments observed in P3 and P4 soil surface.

plant_audit.p1: Stable. The String of Nickels remains consistent in leaf count and color.
plant_audit.p2_p4: Critical. The primary specimen has lost structural integrity; the leaf is chlorotic and collapsing.
plant_audit.p3: Stable. The Pothos leaf remains turgid, though the surrounding soil shows debris.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently moist/dark; no signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3 and P4, likely an intentional soil amendment or incidental debris.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-3: Initial observation shows a healthy green leaf in the foreground pot (P4).
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Progressive chlorosis and downward postural collapse of the P4 specimen.
temporal_deltas.current: The P4 specimen is now almost entirely necrotic/collapsed; the P3 Pothos remains the most resilient occupant.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently experiencing a bifurcated health trajectory. While the Pothos (P3) and String of Nickels (P1) maintain structural and color stability, the specimen in the foreground black pot (P4) has undergone a rapid decline over the 5-day observation window, transitioning from a turgid, green state to a collapsed, necrotic state. The presence of eggshell fragments suggests a potential attempt at soil enrichment or a localized intervention that has not yet arrested the decline of the P4 specimen.
